sections.label: Stanza I
sections.original:
Hēr Æðelstān cyning,
eorla drihten,
beorna bēahgifa,
and his brōðor eac,
Ēadmund æðeling,
ealdorlangne tīr
geslōgon æt sæcce
sweorda ęcgum
ymbe Brunanburh:
bordweall clufon,
hēowon heaðolinde
hamora lāfum,
eaforan Ēadweardes;
sections.translation:
Athelstan
King,
Lord among Earls,
Bracelet-bestower and
Baron of Barons,
He with his brother,
Edmund Atheling,
Gaining a lifelong
Glory in battle,
Slew with the sword-edge
There by Brunanburh,
Brake the shield-wall,
Hew’d the linden-wood,
Hack’d the battle-shield,
Sons of Edward with hammer’d brands.

sections.label: Stanza II
sections.original:
swā him geæðele wæs
fram cnēomāgum,
ðæt hī æt campe oft
wið lāðra gehwæne
land ealgodon,
hord and hāmas.
sections.translation:
Theirs was a greatness
Got from their Grandsires —
Theirs that so often in
Strife with their enemies
Struck for their hoards and their hearths and their homes.

sections.label: Stanza III
sections.original:
Hęttend crungon,
Scotta leode
and scipflotan,
fǣge feollon:
feld dennode
sęcga swāte,
siþþan sunne ūpp
on morgentīd,
mǣre tungol,
glād ofer grundas,
Godes candel beorht,
ēces Drihtnes,
oð sīo æðele gesceaft
sections.translation:
Bow’d the spoiler,
Bent the Scotsman,
Fell the ship-crews
Doom’d to the death.
All the field with blood of the fighters
Flow'd, from when first the great
Sun-star of morning-tide,
Lamp of the Lord God
Lord everlasting,
Glode over earth till the glorious creature
Sunk to his setting.

sections.label: Stanza IV
sections.original:
sāh tō setle.
Ðǣr læg sęcg mǫnig
gārum āgēted,
guma Norðerna
ofer scyld scoten,
swylce Scyttisc ēac
sections.translation:
There lay many a man
Marr'd by the javelin,
Men of the Northland
Shot over shield.
There was the Scotsman
Weary of war.

sections.label: Stanza V
sections.original:
wērig wīges-sæd.
Wesseaxe forð
andlangne dæg
ēoredcystum
on lāst lęgdon
lāðum ðēodum;
hēowon hęreflȳman
hindan ðearle
mēcum mylenscearpum.
sections.translation:
We the West-Saxons,
Long as the daylight
Lasted, in companies
Troubled the track of the host that we hated;
Grimly with swords that were sharp from the grindstone
Fiercely we hack'd at the flyers before us.

sections.label: Stanza VI
sections.original:
Myrce ne wyrndon
heardes handplegan
hæleða nānum,
ðāra ðe mid Anlāfe
ofer ēargebland
on lides bōsme
land gesōhton
fǣge tō gefeohte.
Fīfe lāgon
sections.translation:
Mighty the Mercian,
Hard was his hand-play,
Sparing not any of
Those that with Anlaf,
Warriors over the
Weltering waters
Borne in the bark's-bosom,
Drew to this island:
Doom'd to the death.

sections.label: Stanza VII
sections.original:
on ðām campstęde
cyningas geonge
sweordum āswęfede,
swylce seofone ēac
eorlas Anlāfes,
unrīm hęrges,
flotena and Scotta.
sections.translation:
Five young kings put asleep by the sword-stroke,
Seven strong earls of the army of Anlaf
Fell on the war-field, numberless numbers,
Shipmen and Scotsmen.

sections.label: Stanza VIII
sections.original:
Ðǣr geflȳmed wearð
Norðmanna brego,
nēade gebǣded
tō lides stefne
lȳtle weorode:
crēad cnear on flot;
cyning ūt gewāt
on fealone flōd,
sections.translation:
Then the Norse leader,
Dire was his need of it,
Few were his following,
Fled to his warship;
Fleeted his vessel to sea with the king in it,
Saving his life on the fallow flood.

sections.label: Stanza IX
sections.original:
feorh genęrede.
Swylce ðǣr ēac sē frōda
mid flēame cōm
on his cȳððe norð,
sections.translation:
Also the crafty one,
Constantinus,
Crept to his north again,
Hoar-headed hero!

sections.label: Stanza X
sections.original:
Constantīnus,
hār hilderinc;
hrēman ne ðorfte
mēca gemānon:
he wæs his māga sceard,
frēonda gefylled
on folcstęde,
beslægen æt sæcce,
and his sunu forlēt
on wælstōwe
sections.translation:
Slender warrant had
He
to be proud of
The welcome of war-knives—
He that was reft of his
Folk and his friends that had
Fallen in conflict,
Leaving his son too
Lost in the carnage,
Mangled to morsels,
A youngster in war!

sections.label: Stanza XI
sections.original:
wundum forgrunden,
geongne æt gūðe.
Gylpan ne ðorfte
beorn blandenfeax
billgeslihtes,
eald inwitta,
nē Anlāf ðȳ mā
mid heora hęrelāfum;
hlihhan ne ðorfton,
ðæt hī beaduweorca
bęteran wurdon
on campstęde
cumbolgehnāstes;
gārmittinge,
gumena gemōtes,
wǣpengewrīxles,
ðæs hī on wælfelda
wið Ēadweardes
sections.translation:
Slender reason had
He
to be glad of
The clash of the war-glaive—
Traitor and trickster
And spurner of treaties—
He nor had Anlaf
With armies so broken
A reason for bragging
That they had the better
In perils of battle
On places of slaughter—
The struggle of standards,
The rush of the javelins,
The crash of the charges,
The wielding of weapons—
The play that they play'd with
The children of Edward.

sections.label: Stanza XII
sections.original:
eaforan plegodon.
Gewiton him þā Norðmęnn
nægledcnearrum,
drēorig daroða lāf,
on Dinges mere
ofer dēop wæter
Dyflin sēcan,
and eft Īraland,
sections.translation:
Then with their nail'd prows
Parted the Norsemen, a
Blood-redden'd relic of
Javelins over
The jarring breaker, the deep-sea billow,
Shaping their way toward Dyefln
again,
Shamed in their souls.

sections.label: Stanza XIII
sections.original:
ǣwiscmōde.
Swylce ðā gebrōðor
bēgen aetsǫmne,
cyning and æðeling,
cȳððe sōhton
Wesseaxna land,
sections.translation:
Also the brethren,
King and Atheling,
Each in his glory,
Went to his own in his own West-Saxonland,
Glad of the war.

sections.label: Stanza XIV
sections.original:
wīges hrēmge.
Lēton him behindan
hrā bryttigan
salowigpādan,
ðone sweartan hræfn,
hyrnednębban,
and ðone hasopādan
earn æftan hwīt,
ǣses brūcan,
grǣdigne gūðhafoc,
and ðæt grǣge dēor,
wulf on wealda.
Ne wearð wæl māre
on ðȳs īglande
sections.translation:
Many a carcass they left to be carrion,
Many a livid one, many a sallow-skin—
Left for the white-tail'd eagle to tear it, and
Left for the horny-nibb'd raven to rend it, and
Gave to the garbaging war-hawk to gorge it, and
That gray beast, the wolf of the weald.

sections.label: Stanza XV
sections.original:
ǣfre gȳta
folces gefylled
beforan ðyssum
sweordes ęcgum,
ðæs ðe ūs sęcgað bēc,
ealde ūðwitan,
siþþan ēastan hider
Ęngle and Seaxe
ūpp becōmon
ofer brāde brimu,
Brytene sōhton,
wlance wīgsmiðas
Wēalas ofercōmon,
eorlas ārhwate
eard begēaton.
sections.translation:
Never had huger
Slaughter of heroes
Slain by the sword-edge—
Such as old writers
Have writ of in histories—
Hapt in this isle, since
Up from the East hither
Saxon and Angle from
Over the broad billow
Broke into Britain with
Haughty war-workers who
Harried the Welshman, when
Earls that were lured by the
Hunger of glory gat
Hold of the land.
